item.title: From SaaS Sprawl to a Knowledge Graph
item.description: Turning fragmented identity signals into a foundation for secure automation

item.title: Obsidian Security for the modern SaaS Supply Chain
item.description: Stop SaaS supply chain attacks. Obsidian provides visibility into OAuth integrations, detects compromised APIs, and helps secure non-human identities.

item.title: The new attack surface: OAuth Token Abuse
item.description: Auth token abuse allows attackers to bypass MFA and access data silently. Learn about token theft, session hijacking, and how to strengthen your OAuth token security.

item.title: Obsidian Security Announces Integration with Microsoft Sentinel to Bolster Enterprise SaaS Security
item.description: Obsidian integrates with Microsoft Sentinel to deliver real-time SaaS telemetry, high-fidelity threat alerts, and faster incident response with reduced false positives.

item.title: What is Token Theft? OAuth, Session & API Token Attacks Explained
item.description: Token theft bypasses MFA by abusing OAuth, session, and API tokens. Learn how attackers steal tokens, evade detection, and how to stop token-based attacks.

item.title: What is API Security? Protecting the Hidden Layer Between SaaS Apps
item.description: Learn what API security is and why traditional tools fail to protect SaaS integrations. Discover behavioral strategies to detect compromised credentials.

item.title: What is Webhook Security: Securing SaaS Integrations in 2026
item.description: Learn how webhook security protects SaaS integrations from token theft, lateral movement, and supply chain attacks. Expert guide for SecOps teams.

item.title: SaaS Attack Techniques: How Threat Actors Compromise SaaS Applications
item.description: Learn how attackers use OAuth tokens, session hijacking, and lateral movement to compromise SaaS applications. Expert analysis of real breaches and detection strategies.

item.title: What are OAuth Tokens? How It Works, and Its Vulnerabilities
item.description: Learn what OAuth tokens are, how they enable persistent access without MFA, and their security vulnerabilities. Discover how to protect your SaaS environment.

item.title: What is Account Takeover? ATO Attacks Explained
item.description: Account takeover (ATO) attacks use valid credentials to compromise accounts. Learn 8 attack methods, detection techniques, and response strategies for 2026.
